message:
chore(e2e_capture_search): clone db_path_str in temp_db_ready diag + file two MEDIUM/HIGH semantic-chunk beads

Two tiny correctness-adjacent changes bundled because each on its
own is noise.

**1. `tests/e2e_capture_search.rs` — clone db_path_str before emit:**

The `temp_db_ready` diagnostic payload consumed `db_path_str`
directly:

    emit_diag("temp_db_ready", json!({
        "db_path": db_path_str,
        "temp_root": temp.path().display().to_string(),
    }));

`db_path_str` is a `String` and `json!` moves it, so any
subsequent use of the variable in the test body (for example, a
later `emit_diag` reporting the final search result's parent
database, or a cleanup assertion that re-reads the SQLite header)
triggers a borrow-checker error the moment it lands. The fix is
the one-character change `db_path_str.clone()` so the diagnostic
payload gets its own copy and the original binding remains live.
No runtime impact beyond allocating one extra `String`.

**2. Two new semantic-chunk beads** (pure insertion; 2 lines
added, 0 removed):

- `ft-3zr5f — [HIGH] semantic_chunk_embeddings lacks FK to
  output_segments/panes; prune_chunks_through_ordinal has no
  production caller → orphan vector accumulation`. Review
  finding: the `semantic_chunk_embeddings` table has no
  foreign-key constraints pointing back at `output_segments` or
  `panes`, so when a pane is removed or segments are pruned the
  embeddings linger. The pruning helper
  `prune_chunks_through_ordinal` that would otherwise sweep them
  is not wired up from any production code path, so in practice
  the table grows unbounded over a long session. Filed HIGH
  because it is a latent disk-exhaustion risk for any ft
  deployment that keeps the session DB around for weeks.
- `ft-f11v6 — [MEDIUM] no semantic_schema_version marker; vector
  store not invalidated on storage schema migration`. The
  semantic vector store has no `schema_version` row, so a
  forward migration on the main storage schema does not
  invalidate pre-existing vectors. If an embedding format change
  is ever part of a future migration (e.g., a different
  dimensionality or a different tokenizer), pre-migration
  vectors will be silently searched as if current, producing
  incorrect neighbor results. Filed MEDIUM because no current
  migration changes the embedding format, but this is the hole
  that will matter the first time one does.

Neither bead modifies existing records; both are appended to the
end of issues.jsonl.

files.patch:
@@ -40,6 +40,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3l5bu","title":"[MEDIUM] RateLimiter.pane_counts HashMap leaks entries for every (pane_id, action) pair that is ever checked","description":"# Defect

## File + line

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:2103` — `RateLimiter::check`.

## Concrete failure path

Every `check(action, Some(pane_id))` call executes

    let timestamps = self.pane_counts.entry((pane_id, action)).or_default();

which **always** inserts a `Vec<Instant>` under `(pane_id, action)` if it doesn't exist, before any limit logic runs. Same story for `global_counts` keyed by `ActionKind`.

After a pane is destroyed, nothing removes its entries. `prune_old(timestamps, window_start)` removes expired timestamps from the Vec but leaves the Vec itself and the HashMap entry in place. Over the lifetime of a long-running `PolicyEngine`, the HashMap grows monotonically:

* Bound per pane: at most one entry per `ActionKind` variant (finite enum).
* Bound across panes: `unique(pane_id) × |ActionKind|`.

On a 200+-pane swarm churning through ~1000 panes/day over 90 days, that's ~90k × |ActionKind| entries at ~16 bytes key + baseline Vec — not a crash, but a monotonic leak tracked nowhere. **Same class as ft-l6v1r** (`pane_activity_tracker`) and the `BackpressureMetrics::dropped_by_pane` leak I fixed earlier (f2390da9).

## Worse-case path: pane_id reuse

The ft-l6v1r commit observed that wezterm can reuse pane_ids. If a reused pane_id hashes to an old (pane_id, action) entry whose Vec still contains stale timestamps, the new pane inherits the dead pane's rate-limit budget — either under-counted (if the prune pass has already emptied the Vec) or over-counted (if timestamps haven't aged out yet). The current `check` path prunes based on `window_start` which is relative to `Instant::now()`, so stale timestamps from a destroyed pane will age out correctly — but entries without any timestamps will still mis-attribute the **first** rate-limited hit back to the old pane's history in any diagnostic that reads `pane_counts` directly.

## Fix scope

1. `RateLimiter::cleanup_pane(pane_id) -> usize` — remove every `(pane_id, _)` entry from `pane_counts`, return the number removed for telemetry / debug_assert. Cheap: one full iteration.
2. Unit test pinning: entries present before, zero after, unrelated panes untouched, idempotent on panes never inserted.
3. Wiring into the PaneDestroyed teardown path is a separate follow-up bead — `PolicyEngine` is not currently plumbed through `ObservationRuntime::spawn_discovery_task` or `handle_native_event`, and that plumbing is a broader cross-boundary change that belongs on its own bead.

Single-file scope: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:26.564595Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:31.244870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:31.244127Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-3wow6","title":"[review][coord][LOW] 3 panes editing overlapping surfaces — pane 2 mcp_tools.rs + pane 3 main.rs + pane 4 test-goldens — first-to-land forces rebase","description":"Coordination snapshot at reviewer round 22, 2026-04-23. Three panes currently hold uncommitted edits to files that share the same working tree:

## Pane 2 ft-eu0no (shared PolicyEngine / rate limiter fix for my HIGH ft-eu0no)

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (+77 / -20 hunks) — likely extracting RateLimiter into Arc/Mutex or adding shared-state accessors
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs (+20 / -3) — likely constructing shared engine at server build
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs (+60 / -14, 10 hunks) — rewiring every per-call build_policy_engine site
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs — potentially for audit-record plumbing related to ft-6mmyp follow-up ft-h90rh

## Pane 3 ft-yj375 (wa.state + robot state auth)

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs — CLI entry for ft robot state
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs — regression test

## Pane 4 TOON conformance + ft-2zbie regen

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs — canonicalize + harness updates
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_mission_toon_mcp_conformance.rs (NEW) — TOON parallel harness
- 9 golden_robot_envelope/wa_mission_*.json + wa_tx_*.json — regenerated from placeholders

## Conflict risk inventory

- **mcp_tools.rs** — ONLY pane 2 touching it. No direct conflict with pane 3 or 4.
- **main.rs** — ONLY pane 3 touching it. No direct conflict with pane 2.
- **policy.rs** — ONLY pane 2. Isolated.
- **storage.rs** — ONLY pane 2 (probably).
- **.beads/issues.jsonl** — ALL three panes + this reviewer touch it. JSONL is line-appendable; merges usually clean but can produce duplicate entries or re-ordered lines.

## Real risk: API signature drift

If pane 2's ft-eu0no changes PolicyEngine::authorize's receiver from &mut self to &self (plausible if the rate limiter moves into Arc<Mutex>), pane 3's in-flight main.rs edits may call authorize with the OLD signature. Second-to-land rebase hits a compile break. Compile breaks are recoverable; the risk is the 'verification partial' pattern we've seen 5+ times this session — the second pane may commit without re-running tests post-rebase.

## Concrete coordination step for first-to-land

- Whoever lands first: in the commit message, state the new PolicyEngine::authorize signature explicitly (if changed) so the second pane can rebase mechanically.
- Whoever lands second: re-run the file-specific cargo check before committing. Not the full test suite — just cargo check --lib -p frankenterm-core. Catches any signature drift within ~30s.
- Whoever lands third: same rule.

## Why LOW, not MEDIUM

- No silent-correctness risk; conflicts are compile-time visible.
- The three panes' edits don't mathematically overlap on source lines.
- Filing primarily so a post-hoc audit ('why did three consecutive verification-partial commits land') has a pointer to the coordination window.

Audit round 22, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, coordination.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:59.146818Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:10:02.467910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:10:02.467685Z","close_reason":"Resolved naturally — all three tick-30 in-flight panes landed without the predicted rebase explosion:

- Pane 2 ft-eu0no landed in f540a5d5 (mcp.rs +22, mcp_bridge.rs +118/-, policy.rs +110 — the actual touch set was slightly different from the bead's prediction: mcp_tools.rs got pane 2's rate-limiter threading via ride-along in my subsequent ft-rsqap/ft-mw1zb/ft-zo3cr commits rather than a standalone pane 2 commit).
- Pane 3 ft-yj375 landed in 4d26269f (main.rs + wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs).
- Pane 4 TOON work landed in 62744f55 (mission) and 0f744567 (tx).

Bead's main risk concern (PolicyEngine::authorize receiver changing from &mut self to &self under the SharedRateLimiter refactor) did not materialize — pane 2 kept authorize(&mut self) and wrapped the rate limiter state in Arc<Mutex> without changing the method signature.

What kept it safe:
(1) Natural serialization — panes committed in order of readiness rather than racing, so each landing became HEAD before the next pane's commit went in.
(2) Explicit ride-along flagging in commit messages for files touched by multiple panes (see 810ad4c9, c3d273f0, 97016fe6 all flag pane 2's mcp_tools.rs hunks) preserved attribution when uncommitted work rode along.
(3) Per-pane narrow reservations via Agent Mail avoided compounding blast radius when reservations were flaky.

Remaining open concern (NOT this bead's scope): four recent commits land with 'verification partial' notes citing rch/cargo infra unreliability — f540a5d5, cec75f71, 03eb5831, 8d1e93dc. That's an infra quality issue, not a multi-pane coordination one, and should be its own bead if a separate pane hasn't filed it yet. Separate concern left for ops triage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
+{"id":"ft-3zr5f","title":"[HIGH] semantic_chunk_embeddings lacks FK to output_segments/panes; prune_chunks_through_ordinal has no production caller → orphan vector accumulation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:27:42.036265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:07.118850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","search","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3638,"issue_id":"ft-3zr5f","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

`semantic_chunk_embeddings` (crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs:593-618)
stores raw `pane_id`, `start_segment_id`, `end_segment_id` INTEGERs but has
ONLY a composite FK to `semantic_generations(profile_id, generation_id)`.
There is no FK to `panes(pane_id)` or `output_segments(id)`.

The main schema (crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:104) sets
`PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON`, and the segment-level FK chain cascades on
pane deletion:
  panes → output_segments ON DELETE CASCADE (storage.rs:148)
  output_segments → segment_embeddings ON DELETE CASCADE (storage.rs:162)

But semantic_chunk_embeddings is NOT in the cascade chain. Two explicit
failure modes:

1. `prune_segments_sync` (storage.rs:14157) runs
   `DELETE FROM output_segments WHERE captured_at < ?1`. Cascade cleans
   segment_embeddings via FK. semantic_chunk_embeddings rows still carry
   the now-deleted start_segment_id / end_segment_id values — orphans.
2. Direct pane deletion (pane tombstone) cascades through panes →
   output_segments, but semantic_chunk_embeddings rows keyed on the old
   pane_id stay in the table indefinitely.

The vector store DOES have a retention API:

  chunk_vector_store.rs:381 pub fn prune_chunks_through_ordinal(
      &self, profile_id: &str, generation_id: &str, cutoff_end_ordinal: u64
  ) -> Result<usize>

but:

  grep -rn prune_chunks_through_ordinal crates/frankenterm-core/src/
produces ONLY the definition and two test-mod call sites
(lines 1250, 1268, 1286). No production caller. So in production the
retention path is dead code and stale vectors accumulate forever.

# Why this matters

* semantic_search (chunk_vector_store.rs:399) can return hits pointing at
  deleted segment_ids / pane_ids. Downstream formatters that resolve the
  hit to pane text will either show stale content or error.
* Storage grows linearly with pane churn: every ephemeral pane leaves its
  embedding footprint behind.
* A future migration that drops / renames output_segments (there is no
  plan today, but ft-3681t.x has discussed it) will leave semantic_chunk_embeddings
  with completely dangling foreign keys and no error at startup.

# Shape of fix

Two independent hardens, either is useful:

1. Add FK with CASCADE in the semantic_chunk_embeddings DDL so pane /
   segment deletion transitively cleans the vector store:

     FOREIGN KEY(pane_id) REFERENCES panes(pane_id) ON DELETE CASCADE

   (Cascading via output_segments is tighter but makes the DDL depend on
   storage.rs load order; pane-level cascade is simpler and matches the
   existing pattern.)

   Requires a new migration since semantic_chunk_embeddings is already
   created. SQLite can't add an FK in-place — needs the
   CREATE-new/INSERT-SELECT/DROP-old/RENAME dance. Wrap in a helper like
   the ft-meta rename in storage.rs:4714.

2. Wire `prune_chunks_through_ordinal` into the retention worker that
   calls `prune_segments_sync`. The worker already knows the cutoff
   ordinal per (profile, generation); plumbing is additive.

# Scope guard

Either fix is self-contained. Fix #1 is the durability-first play
(defense-in-depth even if fix #2 regresses); fix #2 is the cheap one
(≤30 LOC if the retention worker already has the ordinals in hand).

# Repro

1. Create a pane, append segments, build a semantic generation.
2. `DELETE FROM panes WHERE pane_id = ?` the pane.
3. Query semantic_chunk_embeddings for that pane_id — rows persist.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3zvmp","title":"retry jitter can exceed max_delay after pre-jitter cap","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:09.235064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.710904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.710537Z","close_reason":"Landed in 464ad9b4 (fix(retry): clamp jittered delays to max_delay)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-45805","title":"[mor:ergonomics] rch PreToolUse hook SIGTERMs cargo; failure mode should be 'fallback', not 'die'","description":"# Observation

From working memory + user experience across recent sessions:

- rch PreToolUse hook (\`force_remote=true\` in
  ~/.config/rch/config.toml) intercepts every Bash call containing
  \`cargo\` and routes to remote workers.
- When remote workers are unhealthy, the subprocess receives SIGTERM
  (exit 143).
- Local fallback exists only via workarounds (Python fork+setsid,
  \`enabled=false\` override, \`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<name>\`
  bypass).

At 3am: operator needs to reproduce a test failure. Runs
\`cargo test foo\`. Shell returns exit 143 with no diagnostic.
Operator has to know the workaround exists. New operators or less-
ranked agents don't know the workaround and waste 30 minutes
re-trying.

This isn't frankenterm code, but it is "the development
environment frankenterm runs inside," and it breaks
frankenterm-the-project's ergonomics during incident response.

# Question

Two levers worth exploring:

1. **Fallback-on-worker-failure in rch itself.** If the remote
   workers SIGTERM the subprocess, catch the signal, run locally,
   mark the worker unhealthy. Exit 143 becomes a last resort,
   not the default failure mode.

2. **Project-level \`just\` or \`make\` target** that wraps the
   known-good build recipe: \`CC=...clang CXX=...clang++
   CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-local-target cargo <args>\`. The 3am
   operator runs \`just test foo\` and it always works — no memory
   of which env vars to set.

Concrete ask: would a one-line \`just build\` / \`just test\`
target in the frankenterm root, with the fork-bypass recipe
already baked in, materially improve the 3am experience? It's
~15 min of work and saves 30 min per incident per operator.

# 3am principle

Every workaround that lives only in memory (mine, yours, the
next-agent's) is a booby-trap for the on-call. Encode the
workaround in a target; when it stops being necessary, the target
becomes a no-op one-liner.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:36:41.686932Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.070631Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:54.611695Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["3am","ergonomics","mor","rch","tooling"]}

 {"id":"ft-eu0no.1","title":"[mcp][test] verify shared PolicyEngine rate limiter regression for ft-eu0no","description":"Run and stabilize the 100-request MCP regression proving the shared Arc<Mutex<RateLimiter>> actually trips the configured limit across requests. Existing test scaffold is in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:mcp_mutation_rate_limit_is_shared_across_requests; this follow-up is for execution/verification, not the structural fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:58:05.632656Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:11:29.244235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:11:29.244053Z","close_reason":"Partial verify in b6c762b5: marked existing shared MCP rate-limit regression ignored pending a repeatable compile lane","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-eu0no.1","depends_on_id":"ft-eu0no","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:58:05.632656Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3625,"issue_id":"ft-eu0no.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocked on verification, not on the mcp_tools.rs scaffold itself. Remote lane: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-Codex-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo test -p frankenterm-core mcp_mutation_rate_limit_is_shared_across_requests --lib --features mcp,asupersync-runtime -- --nocapture -> openssl-sys failed on Linux worker because /opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang does not exist there. Local bypass: FT_LOCAL_NAME=Codex-eu0no scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-core mcp_mutation_rate_limit_is_shared_across_requests --lib --features mcp,asupersync-runtime -- --nocapture -> compile stops before mcp_tools.rs test runs with E0382 in crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs:3536 and :3583 (Arc<MockWezterm> moved into make_pane_executor, then borrowed again). No mcp_tools.rs edit shipped in this turn.","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:08:42Z"},{"id":3626,"issue_id":"ft-eu0no.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Follow-up on the earlier local cargo/test exit-101 blocker: the compile stop in crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs is the moved-value bug previously reported from lines 3536 and 3583 (and the sibling pattern at 3602). Fixed in this turn by cloning the Arc<MockWezterm> before passing it into make_pane_executor(...), so the integration tests can still inspect the mock after engine construction. This unblocks tx-focused test compilation; it does NOT by itself complete the original ft-eu0no.1 rate-limiter regression verification.","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:14:41Z"}]}

Existing test scaffold is in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:mcp_mutation_rate_limit_is_shared_across_requests; this follow-up is for execution/verification, not the structural fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:29.518857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:02:37.503755Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:02:37.503485Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-eu0no.1 (concurrent bead-create race)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-eu0no.2","depends_on_id":"ft-eu0no","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:29.518857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-eze48","title":"[fuzz] add FtviIndex::from_bytes harness for vector index loader","description":"The FTVI vector-index binary loader is public and currently unfuzzed.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/mod.rs:73` publicly re-exports `FtviIndex`.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:218-267` parses attacker-controlled bytes in `FtviIndex::from_bytes`, including magic/version checks, dimension/count headers, and per-record half-float decoding loops.
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no target for `FtviIndex::from_bytes`.

Why it matters:
- This is a binary format loader over persisted search state, so malformed bytes can hit allocation pressure, unexpected EOF handling, half-float edge cases, and count/dimension loops.
- The surface is already public, synchronous, and easy to fuzz without extra seams.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz target that exercises both arbitrary raw bytes and structured valid FTVI buffers via `write_ftvi_vec` round-trips, asserting parse no-panics and round-trip sanity where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331896Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.556262Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.555662Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 6567397b: add FtviIndex::from_bytes fuzz harness","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}
+{"id":"ft-f11v6","title":"[MEDIUM] no semantic_schema_version marker; vector store not invalidated on storage schema migration","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:11.955675Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:28.629408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","search","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3639,"issue_id":"ft-f11v6","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

`ft_meta` (crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs, wa_meta→ft_meta rename
at line 4714) tracks the main storage schema version via `user_version`
and `record_migration`. The vector store in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs` tracks its own
`lexical_schema_version` per generation (line 57) but nothing ties the
two together.

There is NO `semantic_schema_version` / `vector_store_version` row in
ft_meta. If storage.rs migrations change the semantic contract
(for example: change how segment ordinals are assigned, change the
content_hash function, bump the chunk policy implicitly), the vector
store silently retains the old embeddings indexed under the old contract
and continues serving them from `semantic_search`.

Today's scenario is benign because migrations v18/v19/v21 don't touch
the semantic chunk surface. But the guardrail is missing.

# Why this matters

When storage schema bumps, the semantic store should either:
(a) be invalidated (DELETE all rows, mark generation Failed, force reindex),
(b) be version-gated (writes after bump must use the new contract, reads
    from old rows are filtered), or
(c) explicitly documented as schema-independent with a test that freezes
    the coupling.

None of (a)/(b)/(c) is present.

# Shape of fix

Smallest defensive move (≤30 LOC, safe to land):

* Add `semantic_schema_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1` column to
  `semantic_generations` via a new migration.
* On orchestrator startup, if a generation's stored version < current
  const `SEMANTIC_SCHEMA_VERSION`, mark it inactive or Failed with a
  reason string and force a rebuild.

Larger defensive move: add a `semantic_schema_version` row to `ft_meta`
and enforce match at generation register time.

# Out of scope

Actually migrating the contract. This bead is the version-marker only,
so future contract changes HAVE a graceful invalidation path.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:28Z"}]}
